answer:
\boxed{C}

The Egyptian hieroglyphic system **appears to have developed suddenly** (around 3100 BCE) with no clear precursor, unlike the gradual evolution of Sumerian cuneiform. While it incorporated pictographs, it was far more complex than a one-symbol-per-word system (ruling out A and D). The other options (B, E, F, G, H, I, J) are incorrect based on historical evidence.
